segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with an upbeat, adventurous background music as Sonny, a man in a red bandana and black puffer vest, stands before a grand Disney-like castle, introducing himself as a 'FOOD EXPERT' and announcing his challenge to spend over $100 on Disneyland food in Hong Kong. He then "backs up," recalling past visits: first to Paris, where visuals show a bustling street and the 'Disneyland Paris' sign, as he muses about having croissants or crepes with "a s-load of sugar." He then mentions Disney World in Florida, accompanied by a satisfied "mmm" sound, where he had a "giant funnel cake." The scene shifts back to Hong Kong, with Sonny in front of a different castle, confirming they are now at Disneyland in Asia to discover how the food is different. The music continues with a distinct drum beat as Sonny arrives at a food kiosk labeled 'Fine Styles and Selection,' which he identifies as 'Main Street Market.' He observes a young vendor with light-colored hair wearing a hairnet, and describes the "wild array of different foods" in the display case, including turkey legs, waffle marshmallow sandwiches, corn, and "chicken in a plastic bag," all with HKD price tags. Sonny then orders "one giant turkey leg" and "the waffle sandwich thing." A female voice from the kiosk states the total price as "118 dollars," followed by a transaction sound. The video concludes with a close-up of glistening roasted turkey legs in a heated display case, as Sonny explains this is the "hopper where they keep the turkey legs warm," before the audio cuts off.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a bustling Disney theme park, with Sonny, wearing a red bandana, black puffer vest, and gray shirt, excitedly interacting at a food kiosk labeled 'Fine Styles and Selection'. He hands over payment to a vendor in a red uniform, receiving a foil-wrapped roasted turkey leg, which Sonny exclaims is "gigantic" and a "monster," humorously comparing its size to Arnold Schwarzenegger's. The scene shifts as a young vendor in a patterned shirt serves Sonny a waffle ice cream sandwich with pink ice cream, which Sonny calls his "marshmallow sandwich," thanking the vendor and calling him "the best." On-screen graphics display budget tracking, showing '-$7.41' and '$92.59' in a wallet icon. Sonny then walks to a green trash can, placing the turkey leg aside to begin eating the waffle sandwich. Close-up shots highlight the waffle's texture and the pink ice cream, as Sonny describes it as having "pre-made waffles" and a "hockey puck-sized fluffy marshmallow that's pink" and "bouncy." A '+500 calories' graphic appears as he takes bites, aligning with his comment about starting the day with "500 grams of carbs." Amidst the background sounds of park ambiance and upbeat music, Sonny offers a critical review of the waffle sandwich, finding the waffle "a little bit dry" and the marshmallow "confusing," ultimately giving it a "three out of ten" and humorously suggesting it's for kids with less "life experience." The background continues to show colorful theme park buildings, striped awnings, and other guests under a partly cloudy sky, with a 'Main St. Traditional' storefront and Starbucks visible.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of a roasted turkey leg and a waffle ice cream sandwich resting on a green trash can, while an on-screen graphic displays a wallet icon with '$92.59'. A male voice, identified as Sonny, playfully comments on something being "resilient" like a "giant" trampoline, then asks, "What is that made out of?" He expresses being "a little bit offended" as "people keep throwing trash into my table," before correcting himself, "Oh right, the recycling bin." The scene transitions to Sonny, wearing a red bandana, black puffer vest, and gray shirt, standing on a bustling Main Street in a Disney theme park, with the 'Main St. Traditional' storefront and a castle in the background. He introduces, "But next we have a giant turkey leg," and is seen eating the waffle ice cream sandwich while holding the turkey leg. The camera focuses on the turkey leg as Sonny unwraps it, revealing its golden-brown, crispy skin. He exclaims, "It's over $20! That is wild, astonishing, and very expensive," aligning with the on-screen graphic updating to '$72.14' after a deduction of '$20.45' for the turkey leg. Sonny then takes a bite, making eating sounds, and declares, "The turkey leg is delicious. It's smoky." He notes its taste is "very similar to the one that I've had already in Florida," speculating it was "smoked in the US and sent over on a barge." The video includes split-screen comparisons labeled 'USA' and 'HK', with Sonny commenting, "Nice. Hong Kong. So civilized." He continues to eat the turkey leg, pulling off pieces of meat with his gloved hands, showcasing its tender, juicy interior, stating, "I mean, that is a giant piece of meat." Sonny announces, "I'm going to take a big giant bite right here," followed by more eating sounds, calling it "the way to start your day." He observes, "I think people are looking at me. They think I pulled this out of the trash," then clarifies, "But no, I'm just eating on top of the trash. It's so civilized." He rates the turkey leg "like a 9 out of 10," recommending it for those "coming to Disneyland, still trying to bulk." The segment ends with Sonny enjoying his "very satisfying" meal, with the turkey leg partially eaten and the waffle sandwich mostly consumed, as the on-screen wallet graphic remains at '$72.14'.

segments.integrated_caption: The video transitions from Sonny's previous meal to a vibrant, tropical-themed food kiosk with a thatched roof and colorful tribal-patterned pillars, where he seeks a more "Asian-y" food experience. Sonny, still in his red bandana and gray shirt, narrates his discovery of a large, grilled Korean squid, which he deems a "big ticket item." A young vendor in a patterned shirt and red cap operates a specialized orange grill machine, pressing the squid multiple times to create distinct char marks, a process Sonny humorously likens to a "paper shredder" and "Disney magic." As the squid cooks, Sonny notes its "amazing" and "very seafoody" smell, despite joking it looks like it "got ran over by a truck" and is so pungent it would get you "banned" from an office lunchroom. He highlights the "dedicated machinery" used for cooking and slicing. After the squid is placed on foil and handed to him, Sonny takes a bite, reacting positively, while the on-screen wallet graphic updates to reflect the HK$60 ($7.67 USD) cost, which Sonny explicitly states is "$7.67" and justifies by its "lot of protein." The lush, green environment with tropical plants and wooden structures provides a stark contrast to the earlier Main Street setting, accompanied by light background music and ambient park sounds.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with Sonny, a man in a red bandana, gray shirt, and blue pants, enthusiastically holding up a large, orange, grilled Korean squid with distinct grill marks. He describes it in his voice as "amazing squid technology," "chewy," "marinated in soy sauce and sugar," and "delicious," noting its "intimidating" smell but "nice personality," calling it "good beer food" and praising the protein content. This takes place at a tropical-themed food kiosk with thatched roofs and tribal-patterned pillars, while on-screen graphics display a cumulative cost of '$64.47'. Sonny then playfully interacts with a costumed blue rabbit police officer, giving a high-five and smiling, humorously misidentifying the character as "the raccoon from Guardians of the Galaxy," "Rocket," and saying "my friend is a tree," all set to upbeat, playful background music. He gestures towards a nearby structure before walking towards the Tahitian Terrace Restaurant, a Polynesian-style eatery. Sonny announces their arrival at the "Tahitian Terrace Restaurant," expressing his preference for restaurants with pictures on the menu, but noting this one "does one better" with "3D models of all the food." He points to and names "Beef Rendang from Malaysia" and "Butter Chicken" on the canoe-shaped menu display, declaring his intention to order "Asian classics." Moving inside the restaurant, which features warm lighting and wooden decor, Sonny stands in a "hilarious" and long "lunch line," observing the food service area and other guests, feeling like a "Disney slave" waiting "forever."

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens in the bustling kitchen of the Tahitian Terrace Restaurant, where staff in beige uniforms and masks efficiently plate vibrant orange curry and rice. The camera then shifts to the restaurant’s canoe-shaped menu display, featuring Asian specialties like 'Butter Chicken with Coconut Rice' and 'Beef Rendang with Coconut Rice' priced in HKD. Sonny, wearing a red bandana, stands nearby, his voice (American accent) heard contemplating his order, deciding to get both the butter chicken and the rendang, stating, "Couldn't hurt." He then identifies the dishes, saying, "That is the beef rendang, and that is the butter chicken. I'll try it out." The scene transitions to the lush, tropical outdoor dining area, where Sonny is seated at a stone table with two trays of food, including the beef rendang and butter chicken with coconut rice, accompanied by pickled vegetables and banana leaf-wrapped items. Upbeat, traditional-sounding Asian/Polynesian music plays as Sonny remarks, "Alright folks, what you're looking at here is over $40 worth of food, at least according to Disney in Hong Kong." He expresses excitement for a "cultural experience," noting, "the food at Disney in Asia would just be better, and so far, it is." He begins with the beef rendang, describing it as "meat braised in delicious spices and coconut milk," with "varying degrees of softness, gooiness, and gelatinous-ness." He gestures excitedly, then digs in, savoring a tender, saucy piece with his fork. Close-ups highlight the rich curry and fluffy rice, while on-screen graphics track his spending, subtracting $20.45 from $64.47, leaving $44.02. After tasting, Sonny describes the beef rendang as "super salty, more salty than, you know, savory," but praises the beef as "very tender, very soft, you can feel each fiber of the muscle. I like it." He explains that to mix it, "you're supposed to put it with this coconut rice," which he calls "the pride of Malaysia." The lively Polynesian-themed atmosphere continues with other diners enjoying meals under thatched roofs and string lights.

The scene transitions to the outdoor dining area, lush with tropical plants and wooden decor, where Sonny is seated at a stone table with two trays of food: one with beef rendang and coconut rice, the other with butter chicken and rice, both accompanied by side dishes like pickled vegetables and banana leaf-wrapped items. He gestures excitedly as he prepares to eat, then digs into the beef rendang, using a fork to lift a tender, saucy piece, savoring it with evident delight. Close-ups highlight the rich, glossy curry and fluffy rice, while on-screen graphics track his spending, subtracting $20.45 from the initial $64.47, leaving $44.02. The atmosphere is lively, with other diners enjoying meals under thatched roofs and string lights, emphasizing the Polynesian theme of the restaurant.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens at the lush, tropical outdoor dining area of the Tahitian Terrace Restaurant, where Sonny, wearing a red bandana, gray shirt, and blue pants, is seated at a stone table. In front of him are two white trays: one with Beef Rendang with Coconut Rice and the other with Butter Chicken with Coconut Rice, both accompanied by pickled vegetables and banana leaf-wrapped items. Sonny begins by eating the Beef Rendang, describing it in his enthusiastic voice as "coconuty," "very mushy," and "a bit overcooked" as he uses a fork to lift saucy beef and rice. On-screen graphics track his spending, showing a wallet icon with '$44.02'. He then transitions to the Butter Chicken, and the graphic updates to subtract '$20.45', leaving '$23.57'. He takes a bite of the vibrant orange curry, noting its "beautiful orange colored sauce," "rich, heavy," and "vast array of Indian spices," adding that the "chicken is soft and tender" but "super salty," which he jokes makes him want to "buy more drinks." He takes a sip from a Coca-Cola bottle, with a distinct slurping sound. Sonny also describes the pickled vegetables as "purple" and like a "cucumber salad with onions and tomatoes." He then unwraps a banana leaf parcel, initially calling it "mystery food" and joking about plastic, before identifying the steamed item inside as "chicken" with "many pieces... folded on top of each other." He continues to eat, alternating between the two dishes, with close-ups showing the rich textures of the curries and the banana leaf-wrapped side. Other guests are visible in the background, dining at adjacent tables, contributing to the lively, relaxed ambiance of the Polynesian-themed restaurant, with natural lighting and softly glowing string lights.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with Sonny, wearing a red bandana and gray shirt, seated at a stone table in the tropical outdoor dining area of the Tahitian Terrace Restaurant. He enthusiastically eats a piece of Butter Chicken with Coconut Rice, breaking it apart with his hands to reveal its tender, saucy interior. Sonny describes the food as 'good,' noting 'a bit of lemongrass in there,' and finding it 'a little sweet, little savory, and a bit fresh from some of the herbs they put inside.' He states, 'I enjoyed it,' and plans to 'finish the rest of this food and work my way around the park,' adding, 'There is a lot more to see here.' An on-screen wallet graphic shows his spending at $23.57, and he mentions, 'we are getting closer to $100.' The scene then transitions to a wide shot of the Grizzly Gulch roller coaster, accompanied by the sounds of screams, mechanical whirring, and adventurous music. Sonny announces, 'Right now we are at a section of the park known as the Grizzly Gulch,' questioning, 'But what cartoon is this from? Who owns this IP? I'm not sure.' He is seen standing in front of a 'WANTED' poster for 'Rattlesnake O'Riley' before pointing at the menu at The Lucky Nugget. Sonny notes, 'the menu is right here. Everything they have here is fried,' and decides to 'give it a shot.' He selects the 'Fried Chicken with Crispy Fries' for $11.50, and the wallet graphic updates to $12.07. He comments on the 'restaurant so generic they have a food just called Disney Character Pasta Salad,' priced at HK$60, and points out the 'Disney characters' on the pasta, listing 'Mickey Mouse, Mickey Mouse, Corn.' Sonny then walks through the rugged, Wild West-themed Grizzly Gulch area, holding his meal, observing that 'every food place seems to have a theme, and the theme of this one is hot oil.' The segment concludes with a group of guests interacting with a costumed Grizzly Bear character near a shaded seating area with a large umbrella, which Sonny points out, saying, 'Right over there if you look, there is a bear character.'

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with Sonny, a man wearing a red bandana, gray shirt, and blue pants, seated at a stone table in the Wild West-themed Grizzly Gulch area of Hong Kong Disneyland, enjoying a meal of Fried Chicken with Crispy Fries. He takes a bite, breaks a piece of chicken to show its juicy interior, and comments on it being "fresh and hot," "crunchy on the outside, succulent and soft and greasy on the inside," while speculating if it was cooked with fish. He then adds a dollop of "tartar sauce" from a small blue container, which he finds an unusual pairing but declares he "loves it," describing the meal as "classic daycare food" for kids who've "never had fruit or vegetables." The on-screen wallet graphic displays a cost of $12.07, with Sonny remarking they're "getting dangerously close to $100." The scene transitions to Sonny standing and walking through the bustling Grizzly Gulch, which he identifies by name, holding his food. He passes a "little bit scary" Costumed Character (Grizzly Bear) that looks like it "has to go to the bathroom" and still needs to take "about 16 more pictures with kids." He then approaches The Lucky Nugget, a rustic wooden food kiosk with a purple sign and bilingual menu, where guests are queuing, and a glass display case shows items like Disney Character Pasta Salad. An upbeat, Western-themed instrumental music with a distinct "yee-haw" sound effect begins to play. Sonny is later seen near Jessie’s Snack Roundup, a colorful, Western-themed kiosk with a large yellow alien figure and a height chart, which he identifies as being in "Toy Story Land." He notes that the kiosk offers "a lot of food that is maybe not worth reviewing," including "corn dogs." The environment is lively and sunny, with clear skies and a bustling park atmosphere, emphasizing the immersive theme of Grizzly Gulch with its wooden buildings, rock formations, and themed signage like the 'WANTED' poster for Rattlesnake O'Riley.

segments.integrated_caption: The video segment opens with Sonny, wearing a red bandana, gray shirt, and blue pants, standing in front of Jessie’s Snack Roundup, a colorful Western-themed kiosk. He gestures animatedly while speaking in an American accent about food options, noting that "not every food is pushing the limits of creativity" and they need to "keep going till we find something else." An on-screen wallet graphic shows $12.07. The scene then transitions to a wide shot of a whimsical, storybook-style village with a large clock tower, which Sonny identifies as "Frozen Land," as upbeat, whimsical orchestral music begins to play. Guests walk along cobblestone paths in the lively park atmosphere. Sonny approaches Northern Delights, a blue-themed kiosk, pointing to a menu board displaying a 'Coronation Sundae' shaped like Olaf from Frozen, priced at HK$95. He excitedly asks a staff member, Kelli, "Could I have the ice cream that looks like a little snowman?" A cash register beep is heard, followed by a brief, indistinct female voice, likely Kelli. Sonny then exclaims, "Nailed it!" as Kelli hands him the Olaf-shaped soft-serve ice cream in a teal cup. Holding the ice cream, Sonny walks through the bustling area, expressing his satisfaction, "I thought this was all Photoshop, but it's real. My inner 12-year-old is very satisfied right now." He notes, "It's the most expensive ice cream I've ever bought in my life. Yikes!" He then dramatically announces, "Let's start with decapitating Olaf," taking a bite with an audible crunch and an exaggerated "Ah!" followed by "Sorry, buddy." As he eats, the wallet graphic changes to '???'. Sonny continues to enjoy the treat, describing it as "like a marshmallow" and lamenting the "tragic" divot where Olaf's head used to be. He identifies the chocolate chip details as "chocolate or some kind of a chocolate cookie," confirming "It's chocolate, very delicious," and humorously compares the buttons to "charcoal pills that I need to take when I eat street food in India," all while engaging with the camera against the backdrop of the lively park.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with Sonny, a man in a red bandana, gray shirt, and blue pants, standing on a cobblestone pathway in a vibrant, storybook-themed village. He gestures while speaking to the camera, holding an Olaf-shaped soft-serve ice cream in a teal cup, detailed with chocolate chip eyes, a carrot nose, and twig arms. He describes the ice cream as "basically an M&M, a candy-coated chocolate, delicious," and "very refreshing" after savory foods, noting it's even better because it's "personified." The scene transitions to a close-up of the whimsical ice cream, accompanied by upbeat, whimsical background music that continues throughout the segment. Sonny then points out a young child in a Spider-Man costume peeking over a rope barrier, joking, "Nobody tell that kid that Spider-Man is owned by Sony and not Disney." The camera briefly shows a large, animated Mickey Mouse figure holding a smartphone beside a 'STARK' sign with a glowing globe logo, suggesting a Marvel-themed area. Sonny then walks toward the iconic Castle in Hong Kong, its blue and gold spires gleaming under a clear sky, commenting on his on-screen spending tracker, "We're almost at 100. Or we passed it? I'm not sure." He approaches the Royal Banquet Hall, a colorful, fairytale-style structure with purple and teal accents, announcing, "We have come to the Royal Banquet Hall for our final location. This is where we're going to find our most expensive meal of the day." He stops at a glass display case showcasing dishes like Abalone Seafood and Chicken Fried Rice, with bilingual labels, pointing at the food and remarking on the "fake wax or plastic figurines" and expressing disbelief at finding "Abalone here in a Disney Park." He then questions how the "Korean dish" looks in real life before entering the hall with a "whoosh" sound effect. Inside, amidst ornate arches, chandeliers, and a statue of a royal couple, Sonny exclaims, "Oh my god, it's like a refugee camp in here," noting the "different lines: International, Entrees, Japanese, and Grill." He is then served a steaming bowl of Korean Ginseng Chicken Soup with Steamed Rice and a basket of chicken-shaped buns, and is shown seated at a table, eating, with other guests dining in the spacious, warmly lit hall.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with an upbeat, slightly whimsical background tune as Sonny, wearing a red bandana and gray shirt, is seated at an outdoor dining area of the fairytale-style Royal Banquet Hall, adorned with purple and teal accents and stone archways. He excitedly introduces his "absolute final meal," a "mouthful" of Asian-inspired dishes, explaining he'll review them from cheapest to most expensive, starting with the "dim sum." He gestures towards a tray featuring a bamboo steamer basket of adorable Chicken-Shaped Steamed Buns, a bowl of Korean Ginseng Chicken Soup, and a large bowl of Abalone Seafood and Chicken Fried Rice. Sonny, describing himself as a "sophisticated food reviewer" who orders anything "with a face or that looks like an animal or a Disney character," picks up the steamer basket. He humorously notes that while "most of them look good," one bun "looks like it had a botched plastic surgery." He then peels the paper from the bottom of a bun, revealing its savory, dark filling, and takes a bite, making a chewing sound. He expresses surprise and disappointment that there's "no pork in there," identifying the filling as "maybe beans or sugar or some kind of a jam," though he finds the bun itself "soft, moist." Feeling "a little bit ripped off" for the lack of pork, he states that three of these "little piggies" cost "$6.14," aligning with the on-screen graphic. Transitioning, he declares the buns "nothing compared to the Abalone," then scoops a spoonful of the Abalone Seafood and Chicken Fried Rice, describing it as "pretty excellent" with "two whole abalone" on top of a "big heavy rice base," noting that "she poured on this seafood gravy," making it "super savory and sticky." The camera zooms in on the bun's cross-section and the glossy fried rice, while on-screen graphics display the costs of the buns and rice dish, with a wallet icon tracking his spending. The lively background shows other guests dining, including a woman with Minnie Mouse ears, amidst the ornate architecture and ambient lighting of the whimsical Royal Banquet Hall.

On-screen graphics, including a wallet icon with '???' and cost labels '-$6.14' and '-$21.09', track Sonny’s spending, which he comments on, questioning if the 'entire bowl' is 'worth over $21,' estimating about '$3 of Abalone.' segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of a Korean-style meal on a brown tray, featuring a steaming black pot of ginseng chicken soup with whole chicken legs, kimchi, white rice, creamy dumplings, a red Coca-Cola cup, and black chopsticks. On-screen text '-$23.65' and a wallet icon '???' appear. A male voice, identified as Sonny, enthusiastically describes it as "our final dish," "our most expensive meal of the day," and specifically "a ginseng Korean chicken soup." He expresses excitement, noting "bonus things" like "kimchi," "rice," and "tteokbokki," which he clarifies are "Korean pounded rice cakes." The scene cuts to Sonny, wearing a red bandana and gray shirt, seated at a stone table in the Royal Banquet Hall, gesturing excitedly. He picks up a dumpling with chopsticks, noting, "First of all, they put in mushrooms," followed by a chewing sound as he takes a bite and reacts with delight. He then muses, "Usually when you see tteokbokki in Korea, it's on the street and it's in a red sauce made from a red chili paste. Right here, what is this? Alfredo? I have no idea." During this, the video briefly cuts to a vibrant Korean street food market with a vendor and 'KOREA' displayed, accompanied by upbeat, traditional-sounding Korean music, followed by a quick shot of a large wok with dumplings cooking in a creamy sauce, before returning to Sonny enjoying the dumplings. He continues eating, questioning, "Now, is it delicious?" He then concludes, "No, I don't know. I dig that they're doing something new, something different in Korea. I don't think you would ever see something like this," implying the creamy tteokbokki is unusual for Korea. The video transitions to a cinematic night scene of a car driving on a wet bridge with city lights, accompanied by a car driving sound, as Sonny humorously warns, "I think if you did this in Korea, they would throw you off a bridge and then claim that you jumped. You got to watch your back out there." The scene cuts back to Sonny, who pulls a chicken leg from the soup pot, exclaiming, "Alas, a whole flipping chicken! Oh wow. Chicken butt, chicken thighs, chicken back. I'm going to rip off the legs right here," followed by a ripping sound. He takes a bite of the chicken, then uses chopsticks to shred the meat, with the camera focusing on its tender texture and the rich broth. He describes the chicken as "so bland," with "no flavor," and "very fatty," before tasting the broth. He then identifies the dish as "samgyetang," noting he's "had samgyetang many times in Korea" and that "it's kind of a classic." The wallet icon '???' remains visible throughout, and upbeat music continues in the background as he dines in the Royal Banquet Hall.

Upbeat, whimsical background music begins to play. The camera cuts to a close-up of a vendor in a red apron and white gloves serving the soup, with a sign for 'Korean Ginseng Chicken Soup Combo' visible. Sonny comments on the difficulty of mass-producing such a dish, humorously suggesting "Mr. Walt Disney is rolling over in his grave" at the sight of Korean food in a theme park. An overhead shot highlights the vibrant meal before Sonny continues to eat and react with delight. He reveals this is their "final meal" and "the most we've ever spent on a $100 challenge." The scene transitions to an outdoor view of Hong Kong Disneyland Resort, where Sonny walks past the entrance archway, checking his phone and looking around the bustling park, confirming they aimed to spend over $100 at "Disneyland here in Hong Kong." The final shot shows him holding a small, white, chicken-shaped steamed bun, with a digital overlay displaying the cost '$147.50' and a wallet icon with '???', as he builds suspense for the total amount spent, declaring it "the most ever, a record-breaking..." before a humorous interjection about losing his math.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a bustling scene at the Hong Kong Disneyland Resort entrance, showing crowds under a green and red archway with Mickey Mouse atop, as an on-screen graphic displays a wallet icon with '$147.50'. A male voice (Sonny) immediately states, "The amount is an astonishing $147.50." The scene transitions to close-ups of a crispy, lacy, orange-brown snack and a red basket holding a golden-brown fried chicken leg with dipping sauce, while Sonny's voice continues, "I really enjoy the selection of foods that they have here, especially all the different types of Asian foods. The quality of the food, it was okay." Sonny, wearing a red bandana, gray shirt, and blue pants, then appears in front of a colorful carousel, gesturing enthusiastically as he speaks to the camera, stating, "My favorite food of the day was probably the turkey leg. It was made out of meat." He is then seen holding a large, partially eaten roasted turkey leg with foil, before walking away from the carousel area, concluding his segment with, "So that is our video and that is nine different foods you can try next time you come to Disneyland here in Hong Kong. Otherwise, that is it for this one. Thank you so much for watching. I will see you next time. Peace," accompanied by a "peace" sound effect. As Sonny walks away, he humorously adds, "From here, I'm gonna head over to the spinning teacups and throw up all this food. Let's go." The video then cuts to a promotional montage with bold yellow text reading 'IF YOU LOVE' and 'YOU'RE GONNA LOVE OUR NEW CHANNEL', overlaid on a cityscape and food images. Upbeat, energetic music starts, and a different male voice announces, "If you love Indian food, then you're gonna love our new channel, Best Ever Food India." This transitions to a vibrant Indian street food scene, showing a vendor tossing a flatbread in a wok, followed by a man in a blue apron serving a spicy, saucy dish from a stall with Hindi signage. The promotional voice continues over the upbeat music, urging viewers to "Subscribe now for weekly videos, showcasing the most unique street food from around the country." The final frames show a YouTube channel interface for 'Best Ever Food India' with 5B subscribers, featuring Sonny’s face in the banner, and a close-up of a golden-brown, crispy food item being cooked in a wok with steam rising, as the upbeat music fades out.
